column6: This is the taxonomy's own cited absorption: the failed 'minion-phase action ordering' watch item names Spirit Marionette.2 itself as a mandatory-actions ruling, not a phase-ordering one. Direct hit for 3.9's 'mandatory before non-mandatory in the minion phase.'

Discharging the mandatory-bleed obligation by playing a card is 3.9's 'discharge' sub-topic, paired with Spirit Marionette.2 (also moved to 3.9 in this pass). Kept 3.7.1 since the action type is bleed-specific.

Per-copy trigger interaction (only the first Spying Mission triggers per action, later copies don't) is exactly 1.15's 'per-copy triggers' / stacking-cap scope -- the original note itself flags this as the same gap family as Betrayer.3, which is 1.15's founding example. Gap now closed.

3.4's/2.3's 'when the action would resolve' timing window governs why Mask can't be played after Spying Mission, not any substitution of who is acting; 3.10 doesn't apply -- Mask is merely named as a card that CAN'T be used here.

column4: sect as a trait (watch-list) — being non-Anarch doesn't block equipping an Anarch-flavored card, only suppresses the effects that require it; nothing owns sect itself as a gating trait (cf. taxonomy's Galaric's Legacy watch-list entry). 1.6 nearest (requirements to play vs. keep)
column6: 5.9 requires a trait-CHANGE governance question (later effect governs); this ruling is static -- a card's own text gates its EFFECT on a sect trait without gating the equip/play itself. That's an ordinary requirements-to-play-vs-benefit split (1.6's domain), not a precedence dispute. Remains a genuine gap for that reason; 5.9's precedence scope doesn't reach a static play/benefit split.

column4: same sect-as-trait gap — equipping an Anarch-flavored card is not itself "an action requiring an Anarch." 3.7.3 nearest (equip actions) but doesn't own sect-as-trait
column6: Same reasoning as Stolen Police Cruiser.1 (R1733): 'equipping does not count as an action requiring an Anarch' is a static effect-gating fact, not a trait-change precedence dispute; 5.9 doesn't reach it.
1.7 was explicitly widened for this: 'effects naming an amount the target may not hold (steal/transfer templates take what is there).' Direct hit; gap closed.
Mentions 'the acting vampire' but is about ally-recruitment timing (playing an ally directly after unlocking is a separate action), not about substituting who the actor is; 3.10 doesn't apply.

Multiple copies of a non-action card each independently playable/effective within one action is exactly 1.15's 'multiple copies of a card each exerting their effect vs. one combined result.' Gap closed.
Mentions 'acting minion' only to say where a recruited/employed Swarm goes if the action is blocked -- not a substitution of who the actor is; 3.10 doesn't apply.

About whether a burn caused by referendum/side-effect counts as 'the acting minion burning him' for other effects keyed to that phrasing -- an attribution question, not actor substitution; 3.10 doesn't apply.
A mandatory PRESS is a combat sub-step (4.8), not one of 3.9's game ACTIONS (bleed, hunt, etc.); 1.1 already carries the mandatory/optional framing generally. 3.9 doesn't reach it.
'Multiple copies... do not stack... steals only burn the counters on one of them' is 1.15's own 'does not stack with similar effects' language almost verbatim. Same gap family as Betrayer.3.
Same 1.7 widening as Succubus.1 (steal templates take what is there) -- paired instance, same gap.

column4: fixes this card's own printed sect; card-specific fact, no general principle
column6: Fixes this one card's own printed sect -- no trait-change precedence, no stacking, no mandatory action, no actor substitution. None of the expansion's new codes touch a bare printed-sect fact; remains C as before.
Same 1.7 widening as Succubus.1 / Theft of Vitae.1 -- paired instance, same gap.
About which damage SOURCE qualifies (environmental vs. the acting vampire's own damage), not about substituting who the actor is; 3.10 doesn't apply. 4.4 already owns environmental damage exclusions.

'This is a mandatory action' (the choice between hunt and diablerize when empty) is squarely 3.9's core scope ('what makes an action mandatory'), now the more central owner than 1.1's general mandatory/optional framing. Dropped 1.1 as a defensible 4th (noted).

column4: No section owns library-cut mechanics (how a Methuselah may divide/cut a deck being manipulated); rare mechanic, nothing in the taxonomy claims deck-cutting procedure

column6: 6.9 was widened exactly for 'default visibility of card manipulation (library searches ... are not shown)'. Direct hit, paired with Vaticination.1. Gap closed; dropped 3.7.3, which was only ever a gap-filler by action-type, not by content.
Same 6.9 visibility widening as Vast Wealth.6 ('replacement draws are not shown'), the taxonomy's own cited example. Kept 1.9 secondary since replacement mechanics remain its own topic.
About who may play the card (the acting minion's controller), not about substituting who the actor is -- 3.10 doesn't reach this.

Mask is named only as a card that CANNOT be used to continue a canceled combat -- the ruling is about which cards may continue an action as-if-unblocked (3.6) after a cancel (3.5), not about Mask's actor-substitution function; 3.10 doesn't apply here.

column4: Sect-as-a-trait gap (watch-list, per taxonomy): nothing owns sect classification / eligibility for sect-keyed effects like {Anarch Manifesto, An}; 5.7 (traits) and 5.8 (titles/sect change) both touch it, neither claims it
column6: 5.9 requires a trait-CHANGE governance question over time; this states a static classification fact (not Anarch despite flavor/name) with no competing effect changing the trait at a different time. Remains a genuine gap for that reason -- 5.7/5.8 still nearest.

The second half of the ruling (clan checked at unlock time, after the acting vampire's clan has changed) is exactly 5.9's 'whether a changed trait counts... for other cards' triggers' -- same shape as the taxonomy's own Derange.7 clan precedent. Added 5.9; dropped 3.7.5, which here only anchors timing rather than teaching its own principle.

column4: No section states that non-action means of putting a vampire into play (eg. recruit-like abilities) don't count as an "action" for action-keyed effects like {Blood Cult Awareness Network}'s stealth reduction; nearest 3.7.4 (recruit action), though it only covers the action itself
!WORDING mechanics of a two-card combo (Wormwood capping capacity + Orun adding to it); fails transfer beyond this pairing. Nearest 5.1, which doesn't own capacity-stacking between two named cards.
column6: A bespoke two-named-card capacity interaction (Wormwood's own cap + Orun's own text keying off 'that X'), not multiple instances of the SAME/similar effect stacking -- 1.15 owns repeated-effect stacking, not arbitrary two-card capacity math between named cards. Stays C.

This IS the cited founding case for 5.9: 'precedence of a temporary sect override over the underlying sect, and its persistence through a later underlying-sect change' = 5.9's 'temporary override vs. permanent change' verbatim. Kept 5.8 secondary (title eligibility).

5.8 already explicitly claims 'sect change effects on titles' in its own scope line -- already the right home. Not a 5.9 matter: there's no second effect competing to change the same trait at a different time, just the ordinary consequence of a sect change on title status.

Mentions 'the acting minion/vampire' but is about a readiness/state check gating whether the referendum is conducted, not about substituting who the actor is; 3.10's actor-substitution scope doesn't apply.

A used-this-action restriction surviving a Mask-of-a-Thousand-Faces actor substitution is exactly 3.10's 'which effects carry over (action modifiers)' -- before 3.10 existed this had nowhere to go but 1.2. Kept 1.2 secondary (periodicity/once-per-X).

column4: No section owns whether placing a card/counter on a minion opens a "steal" opportunity — a real transferable principle no scope line claims. Nearest 4.10 (equipment theft), but that section is about stealing in combat, not placement triggers.
column6: Not stacking (1.15 governs multiple instances of an effect combining, not a single counter-placement's side effects) and not 1.12 (which owns hosted-card burn/counter-threshold conventions, not steal-opportunity timing). Remains genuinely homeless for that reason.
